  "text": "The JVM bytecode interpreter in HotSpot is implemented across several layers:\n\n## Bytecode Dispatch Table\n\nThe dispatch table mechanism is centered in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `DispatchTable` class and the three global dispatch tables `_active_table`, `_normal_table`, and `_safept_table`. The active table is switched between normal and safepoint variants at runtime.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateTable.cpp`: The base `TemplateTable` class that maps each bytecode to a template (stub of native code).\n- Per-architecture `templateTable_*.cpp` files: Contain the actual assembly-level implementations of each bytecode handler — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateTable_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/templateTable_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/templateTable_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/templateTable_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/templateTable_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/templateTable_s390.cpp`.\n- Per-architecture `interp_masm_*.cpp` files: Implement `InterpreterMacroAssembler::dispatch_next()` and `dispatch_via()` which emit the actual dispatch instruction sequences — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/interp_masm_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/interp_masm_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/interp_masm_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/interp_masm_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/interp_masm_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/interp_masm_s390.cpp`.\n\n## Interpreter Template Generation\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: The shared generator that calls `set_entry_points_for_all_bytes()` → `set_entry_points()` for every bytecode, populating the dispatch tables with generated code addresses.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp`: Emit the actual machine code for method entries and bytecode stubs — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_32.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_64.cpp`, and equivalents for aarch64, arm, ppc, riscv, s390.\n\n## Method Entry Point Setup\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/abstractInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `AbstractInterpreter::set_entry_for_kind()` which registers entry points for different method kinds (normal, native, synchronized, etc.).\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/interpreter.cpp`: Top-level interpreter initialization.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: `generate_method_entry()` dispatches to `generate_normal_entry()`, `generate_native_entry()`, `generate_abstract_entry()`, etc. based on method kind.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp` files each implement `generate_normal_entry()` and `generate_native_entry()` with platform-specific assembly.\n\n## Zero (Portable Bytecode) Interpreter\n\nFor the non-template zero interpreter (used on platforms without JIT support):\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/bytecodeInterpreter.cpp`: Contains `BytecodeInterpreter::run()`, the main C++ switch-based bytecode dispatch loop.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/zeroInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: Generates entry points for the zero interpreter.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/zeroInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture-specific interpreter logic.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/bytecodeInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture bytecode interpreter specializations."
}

conversation_events.text:
# OpenJDK JVM Bytecode Interpretation Loop

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in openjdk/jdk that implement the JVM bytecode interpreter main dispatch loop: the bytecode dispatch table, the interpreter template generation, and the method entry point setup.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the domain domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/jdk--742e735d.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me create the answer.json file.

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of the findings:

## JVM Bytecode Interpreter Implementation

The bytecode interpreter in HotSpot is split across three functional areas:

### 1. Bytecode Dispatch Table
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreter.cpp`** — defines `DispatchTable` and the `_active_table`/`_normal_table`/`_safept_table` globals
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateTable.cpp`** — base template table mapping bytecodes to code stubs
- Per-arch **`templateTable_*.cpp`** — bytecode handler implementations for x86, aarch64, arm, ppc, riscv, s390
- Per-arch **`interp_masm_*.cpp`** — `dispatch_next()`/`dispatch_via()` assembly sequences

### 2. Interpreter Template Generation
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`** — `set_entry_points_for_all_bytes()` populates all dispatch table slots
- Per-arch **`templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp`** — emit machine code for each architecture (x86 has three files: shared, 32-bit, 64-bit)

### 3. Method Entry Point Setup
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/abstractInterpreter.cpp`** — `set_entry_for_kind()` registers entries per method kind
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/interpreter.cpp`** — top-level initialization
- `generate_method_entry()` in the template generator dispatches to `generate_normal_entry()`, `generate_native_entry()`, etc.

### 4. Zero (Portable C++) Interpreter
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/bytecodeInterpreter.cpp`** — `BytecodeInterpreter::run()`, the C++ switch-based dispatch loop
- **`src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/zeroInterpreterGenerator.cpp`** and **`src/hotspot/cpu/zero/zeroInterpreter_zero.cpp`** — zero-arch specifics

    {"repo": "sg-evals/jdk", "path": "src/hotspot/share/interpreter/abstractInterpreter.cpp", "symbol": "AbstractInterpreter::set_entry_for_kind"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/jdk", "path": "src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp", "symbol": "TemplateInterpreterGenerator::generate_method_entry"},

    {"repo": "sg-evals/jdk", "path": "src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86.cpp", "symbol": "TemplateInterpreterGenerator::generate_normal_entry"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/jdk", "path": "src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateTable_x86.cpp", "symbol": "TemplateTable"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/jdk", "path": "src/hotspot/cpu/x86/interp_masm_x86.cpp", "symbol": "InterpreterMacroAssembler::dispatch_next"}
  ],
  "text": "The JVM bytecode interpreter in HotSpot is implemented across several layers:\n\n## Bytecode Dispatch Table\n\nThe dispatch table mechanism is centered in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `DispatchTable` class and the three global dispatch tables `_active_table`, `_normal_table`, and `_safept_table`. The active table is switched between normal and safepoint variants at runtime.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateTable.cpp`: The base `TemplateTable` class that maps each bytecode to a template (stub of native code).\n- Per-architecture `templateTable_*.cpp` files: Contain the actual assembly-level implementations of each bytecode handler — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateTable_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/templateTable_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/templateTable_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/templateTable_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/templateTable_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/templateTable_s390.cpp`.\n- Per-architecture `interp_masm_*.cpp` files: Implement `InterpreterMacroAssembler::dispatch_next()` and `dispatch_via()` which emit the actual dispatch instruction sequences — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/interp_masm_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/interp_masm_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/interp_masm_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/interp_masm_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/interp_masm_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/interp_masm_s390.cpp`.\n\n## Interpreter Template Generation\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: The shared generator that calls `set_entry_points_for_all_bytes()` → `set_entry_points()` for every bytecode, populating the dispatch tables with generated code addresses.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp`: Emit the actual machine code for method entries and bytecode stubs — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_32.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_64.cpp`, and equivalents for aarch64, arm, ppc, riscv, s390.\n\n## Method Entry Point Setup\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/abstractInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `AbstractInterpreter::set_entry_for_kind()` which registers entry points for different method kinds (normal, native, synchronized, etc.).\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/interpreter.cpp`: Top-level interpreter initialization.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: `generate_method_entry()` dispatches to `generate_normal_entry()`, `generate_native_entry()`, `generate_abstract_entry()`, etc. based on method kind.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp` files each implement `generate_normal_entry()` and `generate_native_entry()` with platform-specific assembly.\n\n## Zero (Portable Bytecode) Interpreter\n\nFor the non-template zero interpreter (used on platforms without JIT support):\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/bytecodeInterpreter.cpp`: Contains `BytecodeInterpreter::run()`, the main C++ switch-based bytecode dispatch loop.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/zeroInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: Generates entry points for the zero interpreter.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/zeroInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture-specific interpreter logic.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/bytecodeInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture bytecode interpreter specializations."
}

  "text": "The JVM bytecode interpreter in HotSpot is implemented across several layers:\n\n## Bytecode Dispatch Table\n\nThe dispatch table mechanism is centered in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `DispatchTable` class and the three global dispatch tables `_active_table`, `_normal_table`, and `_safept_table`. The active table is switched between normal and safepoint variants at runtime.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateTable.cpp`: The base `TemplateTable` class that maps each bytecode to a template (stub of native code).\n- Per-architecture `templateTable_*.cpp` files: Contain the actual assembly-level implementations of each bytecode handler — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateTable_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/templateTable_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/templateTable_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/templateTable_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/templateTable_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/templateTable_s390.cpp`.\n- Per-architecture `interp_masm_*.cpp` files: Implement `InterpreterMacroAssembler::dispatch_next()` and `dispatch_via()` which emit the actual dispatch instruction sequences — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/interp_masm_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/interp_masm_aarch64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/arm/interp_masm_arm.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/ppc/interp_masm_ppc_64.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/riscv/interp_masm_riscv.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/s390/interp_masm_s390.cpp`.\n\n## Interpreter Template Generation\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: The shared generator that calls `set_entry_points_for_all_bytes()` → `set_entry_points()` for every bytecode, populating the dispatch tables with generated code addresses.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp`: Emit the actual machine code for method entries and bytecode stubs — `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_32.cpp`, `src/hotspot/cpu/x86/templateInterpreterGenerator_x86_64.cpp`, and equivalents for aarch64, arm, ppc, riscv, s390.\n\n## Method Entry Point Setup\n\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/abstractInterpreter.cpp`: Defines `AbstractInterpreter::set_entry_for_kind()` which registers entry points for different method kinds (normal, native, synchronized, etc.).\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/interpreter.cpp`: Top-level interpreter initialization.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/templateInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: `generate_method_entry()` dispatches to `generate_normal_entry()`, `generate_native_entry()`, `generate_abstract_entry()`, etc. based on method kind.\n- Per-architecture `templateInterpreterGenerator_*.cpp` files each implement `generate_normal_entry()` and `generate_native_entry()` with platform-specific assembly.\n\n## Zero (Portable Bytecode) Interpreter\n\nFor the non-template zero interpreter (used on platforms without JIT support):\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/bytecodeInterpreter.cpp`: Contains `BytecodeInterpreter::run()`, the main C++ switch-based bytecode dispatch loop.\n- `src/hotspot/share/interpreter/zero/zeroInterpreterGenerator.cpp`: Generates entry points for the zero interpreter.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/zeroInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture-specific interpreter logic.\n- `src/hotspot/cpu/zero/bytecodeInterpreter_zero.cpp`: Zero-architecture bytecode interpreter specializations."
}
